knownIssues.title: Windows Mixed Reality was deprecated by Microsoft in 2024
knownIssues.description: Microsoft deprecated the WMR platform in November 2024. The runtime still works on existing installs but receives no updates. New setup on a fresh PC requires archive sources for the WMR installer.
knownIssues.workaround: WMR continues to function on systems where it was previously installed. New installs require downloading the WMR installer from the Microsoft Store archive. This headset should be considered end-of-software-life.

knownIssues.title: Anti-SDE diffuser slightly reduces sharpness
knownIssues.description: Samsung's anti-screen-door-effect diffuser layer reduces perceived screen door effect but also slightly reduces peak sharpness compared to the standard Odyssey.
knownIssues.workaround: No workaround — this is a hardware trade-off. The smoother visual appearance is preferred by most users over the marginally sharper standard Odyssey.

knownIssues.title: Requires HDMI 2.0 — DisplayPort adapters can be unreliable
knownIssues.description: Unlike most VR headsets that use DisplayPort, the Odyssey+ uses HDMI 2.0. Passive DP-to-HDMI adapters do not support HDMI 2.0's bandwidth and will cause issues. Active adapters have mixed compatibility.
knownIssues.workaround: Use a native HDMI 2.0 port on your GPU. Active adapters from reputable brands (Club3D, StarTech) may work but are not officially supported.

optimizationTips.tip: The OLED display delivers excellent contrast — embrace dark VR environments
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: The Odyssey+ screen has true black pixels, making dark scenes and space games look stunning compared to LCD headsets.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: The Odyssey+'s OLED panel provides per-pixel light control with infinite contrast ratio. The anti-SDE diffuser further smooths the subpixel grid. Dark games benefit enormously. Paired with 90Hz, the OLED response time eliminates motion blur visible on LCD panels.

optimizationTips.tip: Use WMR OpenXR for native OpenXR games to bypass SteamVR overhead
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: For games that support it, running directly through Windows Mixed Reality instead of SteamVR gives smoother performance.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Set WMR as the active OpenXR runtime in Windows Settings → Mixed Reality. Native OpenXR games bypass the WMR-SteamVR bridge, reducing compositor overhead by 2-4ms. For SteamVR-exclusive titles, the bridge is unavoidable.

optimizationTips.tip: Adjust IPD precisely — the Odyssey+ has a wide IPD adjustment range
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Take time to get your IPD (eye distance) setting exactly right. The Odyssey+ has good physical IPD adjustment that makes a big difference in comfort.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: The Samsung Odyssey+ supports 60-72mm physical IPD adjustment — one of the widest ranges of any WMR headset. Set IPD using the physical dial to match your actual inter-pupillary distance. Correct IPD reduces eye strain in long sessions and maximizes perceived sharpness through the optics.
